title: Power and renewables 2026 outlook: half-time report
gist: At mid-2026, the strategic divergence between utilities (doubling down on networks and flexible generation) and oil and gas majors (retreating from renewables toward upstream hydrocarbons) has not merely materialized but accelerated and in some cases become irreversible. RWE has emerged as the new global offshore wind leader after BP, EnBW, and TotalEnergies retreated, while geopolitical risk is strengthening the long-term case for renewables, nuclear, and storage in Europe and Asia.

themes.theme: Utilities vs. Oil Majors Strategic Divergence

insights.claim: The strategic divergence between utilities and oil and gas majors that was forecast at the start of 2026 has not only materialized but accelerated and in some cases become irreversible by mid-year.
insights.speaker: Akif Chaudhry
insights.so_what: Investors should treat the utility/O&G divergence as a structural, not cyclical, split — sector rotation out of O&G renewables and into pure-play utilities and infrastructure is a longer-duration trade.
insights.quote: The strategic divergences flagged at the start of the year have not just materialised - they have accelerated, and in some cases become irreversible.

insights.claim: BP, Equinor, and Shell have materially scaled back low-carbon energy commitments in 2026, redirecting capital to upstream oil and gas in a pivot that is described as becoming harder to reverse.
insights.speaker: Akif Chaudhry
insights.so_what: The O&G major exit from renewables is creating asset acquisition opportunities for utilities and infrastructure funds; watch for distressed renewable asset sales from BP, Shell, and Equinor.
insights.quote: BP, Equinor and Shell have materially scaled back low-carbon energy commitments, redirecting capital to upstream oil and gas - a pivot that is becoming harder to reverse.

insights.claim: Aggregate organic investment across leading utilities is set to rise by more than 10% in 2026, with network investment at the core, driven by regulatory support, electrification demand, and transformational M&A.
insights.speaker: Akif Chaudhry
insights.so_what: Double-digit utility capex growth confirms a multi-year regulated infrastructure spend cycle; network equipment suppliers, grid software companies, and regulated utility equities are structural beneficiaries.
insights.quote: Aggregate organic investment across leading utilities is set to rise by more than 10% this year, with networks at the core.

themes.theme: Offshore Wind Consolidation Around Scale Players

insights.claim: RWE has emerged as the new global offshore wind leader in 2026, surpassing Ørsted on contracted capacity following major AR7 auction wins, as BP, EnBW, and TotalEnergies retreated from the sector.
insights.speaker: Akif Chaudhry
insights.so_what: RWE's ascent to global offshore wind leadership following Ørsted's struggles creates a new top-of-market benchmark; Ørsted's relative position has weakened materially and the leadership transition appears durable.
insights.quote: RWE has emerged as the new global offshore wind leader, surpassing Ørsted on contracted capacity following major AR7 auction wins.

insights.claim: Offshore wind is consolidating around large-scale players as mid-tier and oil major developers exit, reducing the number of viable competitors and increasing pricing power for those who remain.
insights.speaker: Akif Chaudhry
insights.so_what: Sector consolidation is typically bullish for the survivors; RWE and remaining large-cap offshore wind pure plays are likely to capture disproportionate share of future auction capacity.
insights.quote: Offshore wind consolidates around scale players: With BP, EnBW and TotalEnergies retreating, RWE has emerged as the new global offshore wind leader.

insights.claim: Investment in gas-fired generation and battery storage is accelerating across the power and renewables sector as utilities respond to intermittency, system balancing needs, and volatile power markets.
insights.speaker: Akif Chaudhry
insights.so_what: Gas peakers and grid-scale battery storage are structural winners in a world of rising renewable penetration; both sectors warrant overweight positioning regardless of the O&G/utility divergence story.
insights.quote: Investment in gas-fired generation and battery storage is accelerating across the sector, as utilities respond to intermittency, system balancing needs and volatile power markets.

insights.claim: Middle East conflict and energy security concerns are strengthening the long-term investment case for renewables, nuclear, and storage, particularly in Europe and Asia.
insights.speaker: Akif Chaudhry
insights.so_what: Geopolitical risk is acting as a structural accelerant for energy independence investment in Europe and Asia; nuclear and storage assets in these regions have improved risk-adjusted return profiles.
insights.quote: Geopolitical risk reshapes strategy: Middle East conflict and energy security concerns are strengthening the long-term case for renewables, nuclear and storage, particularly in Europe and Asia.
